title: Declarative API Validation — Disabling DeclarativeValidationBeta
text:
As a cluster administrator, you might consider toggling
DeclarativeValidationBeta
=
false
under specific circumstances:
Unexpected Validation Behavior:
If enabling
DeclarativeValidationBeta
leads to unexpected validation errors or allows objects that were previously invalid.
Performance Regressions:
If monitoring indicates significant latency increases (e.g., in
apiserver_request_duration_seconds
) correlated with the feature's enablement.
High Mismatch Rate:
If the
declarative_validation_mismatch_total
metric shows frequent mismatches, suggesting potential bugs in the declarative rules affecting the cluster's workloads, even if
DeclarativeValidationBeta
is false.
To revert
+k8s:beta
validation rules back to shadow mode, disable the
DeclarativeValidationBeta
feature gate, for example via command-line arguments: (
--feature-gates=DeclarativeValidationBeta=false
).
